message.content:
Test file: src/game-of-life.spec.ts
Activate test: "should kill a live cell with more than 3 neighbors (overpopulation)"
Current state: 5 tests passing, 3 todo
Implementation file: src/game-of-life.ts

The implementation already has the full Game of Life rules (survival with 2-3 neighbors, reproduction with exactly 3). The overpopulation rule (cells with >3 neighbors die) is implicitly handled — the condition `liveNeighborCount === 3 || (alive && liveNeighborCount === 2)` already excludes cells with 4+ neighbors.

Use the spec example: a cross/plus pattern where center cell has 4 neighbors:
Input: [[0,1], [1,0], [1,1], [1,2], [2,1]] — plus shape
Center cell (1,1) has 4 live neighbors → dies (overpopulation)

This test will likely PASS already since the implementation correctly excludes cells with 4+ neighbors.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Green after completion.
You MUST output the full Step 7 block verbatim (both Compilation
Prediction and Runtime Prediction lines, each with Correct or
Incorrect). Do not abbreviate. Do not collapse the two prediction
lines into one.
